old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.7","title":"Authenticate multi-hop DAO origins","description":"Non-Storing DAO forwarding preserves the origin IPv6 source while each relay replaces the link-layer signature. Hop authentication therefore cannot prove DAO origin at the root; an authenticated relay can forge another source/target and Path Sequence. Design and implement cryptographically preserved end-to-end DAO provenance, bound to RPLInstanceID, DODAGID, DAOSequence, ordered Target/Transit options, and origin IPv6 identity, without breaking hop forwarding.","design":"Do not pretend immediate-hop link signatures authenticate the end-to-end IPv6 source. Candidate mechanisms are an origin signature option or OSCORE-protected DAO envelope; choose only after wire/spec and cross-implementation review.","acceptance_criteria":"A documented wire format and trust model identify the origin key; root verifies provenance before DAO replay/path/route mutation; relays can forward without resigning origin content; replay, target substitution, option reordering, withdrawal forgery, and unknown-key tests fail closed; C/Python/Rust protocol/spec/vector implications are tracked; three security reviews pass.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-17T22:08:32.40085Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-17T22:08:32.40085Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.7","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-17T22:08:32.530045Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.7","title":"Authenticate multi-hop DAO origins","description":"Non-Storing DAO forwarding preserves the origin IPv6 source while each relay replaces the link-layer signature. Hop authentication therefore cannot prove DAO origin at the root; an authenticated relay can forge another source/target and Path Sequence. Design and implement cryptographically preserved end-to-end DAO provenance, bound to RPLInstanceID, DODAGID, DAOSequence, ordered Target/Transit options, and origin IPv6 identity, without breaking hop forwarding.","design":"Do not pretend immediate-hop link signatures authenticate the end-to-end IPv6 source. Candidate mechanisms are an origin signature option or OSCORE-protected DAO envelope; choose only after wire/spec and cross-implementation review.","acceptance_criteria":"A documented wire format and trust model identify the origin key; root verifies provenance before DAO replay/path/route mutation; relays can forward without resigning origin content; replay, target substitution, option reordering, withdrawal forgery, and unknown-key tests fail closed; C/Python/Rust protocol/spec/vector implications are tracked; three security reviews pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Mark Atwood","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-17T22:08:32.40085Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-19T23:39:11.491363Z","started_at":"2026-07-19T23:39:11.491363Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-19T23:44:11.491363Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-19T23:39:11.491363Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.7","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-17T22:08:32.530045Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"design":"Implement the approved terminal provisional DAO Origin Signature option using existing Schnorr48 and pinned Announce identities. The option is unique and final, carries a persistent 64-bit origin sequence plus 48-byte signature, and signs a domain-separated SHA-512 transcript binding origin IPv6, effective DODAGID, origin sequence, and exact unsigned DAO bytes. Relays preserve it unchanged. Roots verify key/IID, signature, and persistent sequence before parsing authorization-sensitive semantics or mutating replay/routes. Exact static prefix delegations are handled by .44.9.2 after provenance.","notes":"2026-07-19 user approved Schnorr48 terminal DAO origin option and exact static prefix delegation policy. OSCORE rejected because DAO is ICMPv6; symmetric Secure DAO MAC rejected because it conflicts with LICHEN public-key/no-PSK trust model."}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented in /Volumes/Attic/Opencode/opencode/lichen-rust-rpl on integration/rust-rpl-recovery. Added terminal Schnorr48 DAO origin envelope, pinned Announce authorization, stable-key persistent replay high-water, atomic two-slot NonVolatile storage, reserve-before-sign TX state, verify-before-mutation RX flow, relay byte preservation, and fixed vectors. Final validation 2026-07-19: lichen-rpl 103 unit + 2 vector + 5 integration tests pass; lichen-node 160 unit + 1 vector pass; lichen-hal 9 pass; relevant clippy/no-default/diff checks pass. Three security review passes completed. Known unrelated no_std node failures remain tracked by .60."}
Implemented and validated end-to-end DAO origin authentication

new_value: {"notes":"Completed on integration/rust-rpl-complete. Reconciled prefix/path-snapshot model and added production RplStack ownership for radio, RPL, Announce trust, scope-bound DTX2/DRX2/DAD1 persistence, explicit bounded DAO admission, transactional multi-hop Announce relay, DIO/DIS/Trickle control, preferred-parent DAO forwarding, strict IPv6/ICMPv6 and L2 destination validation, durable replay ordering, prefix-egress repair, and exact failure/restart behavior. External 50-vector node test remains intact. Three final independent release reviews found no P0-P2 issues. Validation: Rust lichen-link 29+2+1, lichen-ipv6 13, lichen-rpl 125+2+5, lichen-node 185+2+docs pass; scoped strict Clippy passes except pre-existing scheduler test helper blocks all-target node Clippy; Python DAO vectors 54 pass; independent C/Monocypher oracle 50 pass; Zephyr native_sim/native/64 link_crypto 10/10 pass with explicit ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES.
